channel.title: Projects on Home
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Home

item.title: Battlesnake 2019
item.description: A simple Battlesnake AI written in Python. This snake was forked from the starter snake repo and deployed to Heroku for the competition. Check out my snake and others at the Battlesnake Arena! Here’s my snake in a battle. I’m the purple one! My Snake In Action This little guy made it to round 2 of the competiton!

item.title: Real-Time Embedded System Project: Traffic Light System
item.description: Real-Time Traffic Light System A simulated traffic light system built using FreeRTOS and an STM32 Microcontroller for ECE 455: Real-Time Operating Systems course at UVic. Source Code Requirements Build a one-way traffic light system which simulates cars as green LEDs being lit up sequentially using a shift register IC. The traffic density will be determined by reading the Analog to Digital Converter (ADC) of the microcontroller. The ADC will be connected to a potentiometer.

item.title: Reqcheck: Course Prerequisite Visualization App
item.description: Reqcheck.tech : A Web Application for Prerequisite Course Visualization Reqcheck.tech You can view the source code on Github

item.title: Two Player Tetris Using Pygame and Sockets
item.description: Introduction The following report will describe the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) for 2-Player Tetris built for ECE 356: Engineering System Software. The five main steps in the SDLC are Requirement Engineering, Design, Implementation, Testing, and Maintenance. The purpose of creating this game was to become familiar with basic client/server architecture. The result is a 2-player game with network functionality using Python. The source code can be found here.The process of creating this game was both challenging and rewarding.

item.title: Hack UVic Twitter Bot
item.description: Golden Hour: notifications for upcoming sunrise and sunset conditions A simple twitter bot written in Python using the Twitter API and openweathermap API.
